visualizerParams.title: Using `math.ceil()` Built-in
visualizerParams.problemDescription: The provided code manually implements the ceiling logic using type casting and comparisons. Refactor it to use Python's built-in `math.ceil()` function, which is the standard, most efficient, and reliable way to perform this operation.

solution.explanation: Python's `math.ceil()` is implemented in C and handles all edge cases (like extremely large floats or negative values) correctly and efficiently. Using standard library functions is preferred for clarity and performance.
